issues.title: get_available_slots uses DD-MM-YYYY format but find_appointment and find_patient use YYYY-MM-DD
issues.description: The get_available_slots tool defines start_date as DD-MM-YYYY format, while find_patient uses YYYY-MM-DD and find_appointment uses YYYY-MM-DD for date_from/date_to. The prompt never mentions this format discrepancy or instructs the agent to format dates differently per tool. In production, the agent will almost certainly pass YYYY-MM-DD to get_available_slots (consistent with the other tools), causing silent API failures or incorrect slot results. This is especially dangerous because no error handling is described for a failed slot search.
issues.evidence: "start_date": { "type": "string", "description": "Start date for search window in DD-MM-YYYY format" }
issues.location_hint: get_available_slots tool definition, start_date parameter

issues.title: Rescheduling cancels old appointment before confirming new slot exists, with no rollback
issues.description: The rescheduling workflow instructs the agent to cancel the old appointment first using cancel_appointment before a new appointment is successfully booked. If get_available_slots returns no results, or if book_appointment fails, the patient has lost their original appointment with no way to restore it. There is no rollback instruction or contingency path described for this failure scenario.
issues.evidence: "Cancel the old appointment first using cancel_appointment. The same cancellation policy applies — if it is within 24 hours, inform them of the possible fee. Then schedule a new appointment."
issues.location_hint: For rescheduling appointments section

issues.title: No error handling defined for any tool call failure
issues.description: The prompt defines no instructions for what the agent should do when any tool call returns an error, times out, or returns unexpected data. For book_appointment, cancel_appointment, find_patient, and send_sms, silent failures would leave patients with incorrect beliefs about their appointment status. There is no fallback such as 'if booking fails, inform the patient and offer to transfer to front desk.'
issues.evidence: Tools available: find_patient, find_appointment, get_available_slots, book_appointment, cancel_appointment, confirm_appointment, transfer_call, send_sms, end_call
issues.location_hint: Introduction / Tools available section — absence of error handling throughout

issues.title: get_available_slots lacks end_date parameter, making search window undefined
issues.description: The get_available_slots tool only accepts a start_date, with no end_date or window_days parameter. The prompt instructs the agent to search 'the next 30 days' and 'the next week,' but there is no way to constrain the search window via the tool. The API will return a server-default window, which may produce too many results or results spanning months, making the two-option presentation logic unreliable.
issues.evidence: "If there are no available slots at all for their preferred provider in the next 30 days, let them know and offer alternatives." and "start_date": { "type": "string", "description": "Start date for search window in DD-MM-YYYY format" }
issues.location_hint: get_available_slots tool definition and scheduling workflow section

issues.title: Waitlist functionality referenced but no tool or mechanism exists to record it
issues.description: The prompt instructs the agent to 'put them on a waitlist' and 'take their preferred provider, date range, and time of day preference and make a note' in two separate scenarios, but no tool exists for this. There is no waitlist tool, no notes tool, and no instructions to use send_sms or book_appointment to record waitlist intent. The agent will either hallucinate a capability or silently fail to record the patient's request.
issues.evidence: "offer to put them on a waitlist — take their preferred provider, date range, and time of day preference and make a note." and "offer to be added to the waitlist for Dr. Chen"
issues.location_hint: Scheduling workflow — slot search section and no-availability section

issues.title: Cancellation workflow does not send SMS confirmation after canceling
issues.description: The scheduling and rescheduling workflows both include send_sms steps, but the standalone cancellation workflow has no instruction to send an SMS confirmation after cancel_appointment succeeds. The send_sms tool supports message_type 'appointment_cancelled' explicitly, indicating this is an intended capability. Patients who cancel will not receive written confirmation of their cancellation.
issues.evidence: "If they say yes, use cancel_appointment." — no subsequent send_sms instruction follows in the cancellation section.
issues.location_hint: For canceling appointments section

issues.title: Phone number format for find_patient is unspecified, likely causing lookup failures
issues.description: The find_patient tool accepts a phone parameter but gives no format specification. The prompt instructs the agent to try the caller's phone number (available as {{user_number}}) as a third-step fallback, but {{user_number}} format (e.g., +1XXXXXXXXXX vs 10-digit vs formatted) may not match the EHR's expected format. No normalization instruction is given, and a format mismatch will cause silent no-match results on what may be the last viable verification attempt before transferring.
issues.evidence: "ask if the phone number they are calling from is the one on file or if there is a different number. Try again with the phone number." and "phone": { "type": "string", "description": "Patient phone number" }
issues.location_hint: Patient verification section and find_patient tool definition

issues.title: 3-year new patient rule not enforced — agent not instructed to check last_visit_date
issues.description: The prompt instructs the agent to treat patients not seen in over 3 years as new patients requiring a new patient visit, but the scheduling workflow does not instruct the agent to inspect the last_visit_date field returned by find_patient and apply this rule. If an existing patient has a record but was last seen 4 years ago, the agent will verify them normally and proceed with scheduling a follow-up or other visit type rather than routing them as a new patient.
issues.evidence: "If a patient has not been seen in over 3 years, they need to be scheduled as a new patient even if they are already in the system. The system may still have their record but they need a new patient visit to update everything."
issues.location_hint: Scheduling rules section

issues.title: 3-appointment limit cannot be checked — agent has no instruction to use find_appointment to verify count before booking
issues.description: The prompt states 'Patients can have up to 3 upcoming appointments at a time. If they already have 3, let them know and ask if they want to cancel one or speak with the front desk.' However, the scheduling workflow does not instruct the agent to call find_appointment before booking to count existing upcoming appointments. The agent will proceed directly to get_available_slots and book_appointment without enforcing this business rule.
issues.evidence: "Patients can have up to 3 upcoming appointments at a time. If they already have 3, let them know and ask if they want to cancel one or speak with the front desk."
issues.location_hint: Scheduling rules section

issues.title: late_cancel flag not instructed to be set in cancel_appointment call when within 24 hours
issues.description: The cancel_appointment tool has a late_cancel boolean parameter. The prompt describes the 24-hour cancellation policy and says to inform the patient of a possible fee, but never instructs the agent to pass late_cancel: true when canceling within 24 hours. This means the cancellation fee flag will never be set in the system, causing billing to miss chargeable late cancellations.
issues.evidence: "If they are canceling same-day or with less than 24 hours notice, let them know there may be a cancellation fee." — no instruction to set late_cancel: true in the cancel_appointment call.
issues.location_hint: Cancellation policy section and cancel_appointment tool definition

issues.title: No guard instruction to prevent calling get_available_slots for Dr. Patel during maternity leave
issues.description: The prompt instructs the agent not to schedule Dr. Patel from March 15 through June 1 2026, but there is no explicit instruction to avoid calling get_available_slots for Dr. Patel during that window. If the scheduling system has not blocked those slots, or if the agent misidentifies the date boundary, it may present and book slots with her during her leave period.
issues.evidence: "She is on maternity leave starting March 15 2026 and returning June 1 2026. During her leave, her patients should be scheduled with Dr. Chen."
issues.location_hint: Provider information section — Dr. Priya Patel

issues.title: Provider IDs and location IDs are never defined — agent cannot correctly populate required fields in get_available_slots and book_appointment
issues.description: The get_available_slots and book_appointment tools require provider_id and location_id as strings, and both are marked required. The prompt describes providers and locations by name but never provides their system ID values. The agent must infer or hallucinate IDs such as 'provider_id for Dr. Chen' or 'location_id for Main Office,' which will cause API failures on every booking attempt.
issues.evidence: "provider_id": { "type": "string", "description": "Provider ID — required to search for a specific provider's slots" }" and "location_id": { "type": "string", "description": "Location ID where the appointment should be held" }
issues.location_hint: get_available_slots and book_appointment tool definitions — no ID mapping table in prompt

issues.title: Urgent visit same-day enforcement relies solely on agent reasoning with no date-constraining mechanism in the tool
issues.description: Urgent visits are same-day only and cannot be scheduled in advance. However, the agent is not explicitly instructed to derive today's date from {{current_time_America/New_York}} and use it as the start_date when calling get_available_slots for urgent visits. Without this explicit instruction, the agent may pass an incorrect or future date. Additionally, since get_available_slots has no end_date parameter, there is no way to constrain results to today only via the tool itself.
issues.evidence: "Urgent Visit: 15 minutes. Same-day only. Can only be booked with PA Marcus Davis. For issues that need immediate attention but are not emergencies...Cannot be scheduled in advance."
issues.location_hint: Appointment types section — Urgent Visit

issues.title: Lab review ordering provider rule cannot be enforced — no tool field exposes ordering provider data
issues.description: The prompt instructs the agent to 'schedule a lab review with the ordering provider only' and to correct patients who request the wrong doctor. However, no tool returns an ordering_provider or lab_order_provider field. The agent must rely entirely on what the patient states, which defeats the guard condition entirely.
issues.evidence: "Lab review appointments have to be with the doctor who ordered the labs. If they say 'I want to review my bloodwork with Dr. Torres' but Dr. Chen ordered the labs, you need to let them know and schedule with Dr. Chen instead."
issues.location_hint: Scheduling rules section — lab review rule

issues.title: Referring provider instructed to be placed in notes field instead of the dedicated referring_provider parameter
issues.description: The prompt says 'make a note of the referring provider's name when booking. Include it in the notes field.' But book_appointment has a dedicated referring_provider field separate from notes. The prompt instructs misuse of the notes field for referring provider data instead of the correct parameter, causing structured data to be stored as unstructured text.
issues.evidence: "If a patient says they were referred by another doctor, make a note of the referring provider's name when booking. Include it in the notes field."
issues.location_hint: Additional scheduling guidelines section — referral note instruction

issues.title: Confirm appointment workflow does not send SMS confirmation after confirming
issues.description: After using confirm_appointment, the prompt says 'Your appointment has been confirmed. We look forward to seeing you.' but gives no instruction to call send_sms with message_type 'appointment_confirmed'. The send_sms tool explicitly supports this message type, and patients expect written confirmation when they confirm verbally.
issues.evidence: "use confirm_appointment and say 'Your appointment has been confirmed. We look forward to seeing you.'" — no send_sms instruction follows.
issues.location_hint: For confirming appointments section

issues.title: 6-month advance booking limit has no enforcement mechanism
issues.description: The prompt states 'Do not schedule appointments more than 6 months in advance,' but there is no instruction on how to calculate or enforce this limit using available tools. get_available_slots has no max_date parameter, and the agent is not instructed to derive a 6-month cutoff from {{current_time_America/New_York}} and filter returned slots against it.
issues.evidence: "Do not schedule appointments more than 6 months in advance."
issues.location_hint: Scheduling rules section

issues.title: send_sms after rescheduling uses wrong message_type — no instruction to use appointment_rescheduled
issues.description: The send_sms tool defines a dedicated message_type of 'appointment_rescheduled', but the rescheduling workflow only instructs the agent to book a new appointment and send a confirmation. There is no instruction to use the appointment_rescheduled message type, and the agent will likely default to appointment_booked, producing a misleading message to the patient that omits context about the cancellation of the prior appointment.
issues.evidence: "Then schedule a new appointment. Ask when they would like to come in. Use get_available_slots to find openings. Offer them two times. If they pick one, confirm and book with book_appointment." — no send_sms with appointment_rescheduled instruction follows.
issues.location_hint: For rescheduling appointments section — post-booking step

issues.title: cancelled_by parameter never instructed to be set in cancel_appointment, breaking audit trail
issues.description: The cancel_appointment tool defines a cancelled_by field ('patient, provider, office') that, while not marked required, is clearly important for billing and audit purposes. The prompt never instructs the agent to set this field to 'patient' for patient-initiated cancellations. All cancellations processed by the agent will be missing this attribution.
issues.evidence: "cancelled_by": { "type": "string", "description": "Who is cancelling: patient, provider, office" }
issues.location_hint: cancel_appointment tool definition — field never referenced in prompt

issues.title: Distressed, angry, or emotionally escalated callers have no defined de-escalation or escalation pathway
issues.description: The prompt only offers two generic phrases for upset patients: 'I understand' or 'I apologize for the inconvenience.' There is no guidance for callers who are frightened, grieving, panicked, or persistently angry, and no escalation path to a supervisor or office manager is defined for situations where the agent's responses are not resolving the caller's distress.
issues.evidence: "If the patient gets frustrated or seems upset, say 'I understand' or 'I apologize for the inconvenience' and try to help them."
issues.location_hint: General instructions section — emotional handling

issues.title: Prohibition on contractions creates unnatural robotic speech throughout all interactions
issues.description: The instruction to never use contractions will make the agent sound stilted and robotic in natural phone conversation. Contractions are a normal feature of spoken American English and their absence is conspicuous and off-putting, undermining the goal of a warm, professional interaction. This is especially problematic during empathetic moments.
issues.evidence: "Do not use contractions like 'I'm' or 'can't'. Say 'I am' and 'cannot'."
issues.location_hint: Speech style instructions section

issues.title: Provider biographical details included with no guidance on when or how to share them
issues.description: The prompt includes extensive provider background data (years with practice, star ratings, degrees, specialties, patient comments) but gives no instruction on when or how to share this information conversationally. Without guidance, the agent may dump all provider details unprompted or never use them, and there is no instruction to use these details to help hesitant patients make a provider choice.
issues.evidence: "She has a 4.9 star patient satisfaction rating. Her patients frequently comment on her thoroughness and bedside manner. She has been with the practice for 12 years."
issues.location_hint: Provider information section — Dr. Sarah Chen

issues.title: Cancellation fee warning script is not calibrated to patient emotional state or hardship reasons
issues.description: The scripted cancellation fee warning is delivered as a policy recitation regardless of context. A patient canceling because of a family emergency or their own illness will experience this as cold and punitive. There is no instruction to adjust tone when a patient explains a hardship reason for late cancellation, nor any guidance to offer empathy before stating the policy.
issues.evidence: "Say something like 'I can certainly cancel that for you. I do want to let you know that since the appointment is within 24 hours, there may be a late cancellation fee per our office policy. Would you still like to proceed with the cancellation?'"
issues.location_hint: Cancellation policy section

detection.analysis_notes: Changes made: REMOVED WA-06 (send_sms patient_id scope) — the patient_id is obtained during verification at the start of every workflow and there is no realistic multi-turn mechanism that would clear it; the claim about appointment_id is speculative. REMOVED WA-12 (cancelled_by audit trail) — this was substantively identical to the retained WA-22, which is a cleaner formulation; kept WA-22 instead. REMOVED WA-17 (pharmacy/records destinations unused) — this is a stylistic/dead-code observation about the tool definition, not a prompt quality issue; the transfer destinations the prompt does reference all map correctly to defined values. REMOVED PE-01 (new patient routing abrupt) — the scripted language is functional and appropriate for a front-desk voice agent; the critique is a stylistic preference. REMOVED PE-03 (verification is interrogative) — the sequential name/DOB collection is standard medical office practice and appropriate for a voice agent; this is a stylistic preference. REMOVED PE-04 (no sub-emergency distress response) — this is speculative and overlaps with PE-02, which covers the broader gap in emotional handling. REMOVED PE-07 (hold language perfunctory) — purely stylistic. REMOVED PE-08 (cancellation fee tone) — folded into retained PE-11 as a cleaner version of the same issue. REMOVED PE-09 (persona too thin) — stylistic preference. REMOVED PE-10 (slot presentation rigid) — stylistic preference. REMOVED PE-11 original numbering conflict — renumbered to PE-11 for cancellation fee issue. ADDED WA-21 (rescheduling workflow missing appointment_rescheduled message_type instruction) — the send_sms tool has a dedicated message_type for rescheduling that the prompt never references, which is a concrete tool-usage gap. ADDED WA-22 (cancelled_by field) — retained from WA-12 with improved framing. WA-09 was revised to be more precise: the issue is not that the rule is 'unverifiable' (last_visit_date IS returned by find_patient) but that the scheduling workflow never instructs the agent to check it. This is a more accurate and actionable framing.

proposals.root_cause: The get_available_slots tool requires DD-MM-YYYY format for start_date, but the agent naturally uses YYYY-MM-DD consistent with the other tools, creating a silent format mismatch.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Every slot search will fail or return wrong results, making scheduling impossible.
proposals.fix_description: Add an explicit instruction in the scheduling workflow to format the start_date as DD-MM-YYYY when calling get_available_slots.
proposals.original_text: Now you need to find available times. Use get_available_slots to search for openings.
proposals.replacement_text: Now you need to find available times. Use get_available_slots to search for openings. When calling get_available_slots, format the start_date as DD-MM-YYYY (for example, June 5th 2026 becomes 05-06-2026).

proposals.root_cause: The rescheduling workflow cancels the old appointment before confirming a new slot exists, leaving the patient with no appointment if booking fails.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Patients can lose their original appointment with no way to recover it if no new slot is available or booking fails.
proposals.fix_description: Reverse the order: find and confirm the new slot before canceling the old appointment.
proposals.original_text:
Cancel the old appointment first using cancel_appointment. The same cancellation policy applies — if it is within 24 hours, inform them of the possible fee.

Then schedule a new appointment. Ask when they would like to come in. Use get_available_slots to find openings. Offer them two times. If they pick one, confirm and book with book_appointment.
proposals.replacement_text: First, schedule the new appointment. Ask when they would like to come in. Use get_available_slots to find openings. Offer them two times. Once they have chosen a new time and you have confirmed the details with them, then cancel the old appointment using cancel_appointment. The same cancellation policy applies — if it is within 24 hours, inform them of the possible fee. If no suitable new slot can be found, do not cancel the old appointment — instead inform the patient and offer to transfer them to the front desk. Then book the new appointment with book_appointment.

proposals.root_cause: No instructions exist for what the agent should do when any tool call returns an error or unexpected result.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Silent failures leave patients with incorrect beliefs about their appointment status, causing missed appointments and patient harm.
proposals.fix_description: Add a general error handling instruction after the tools list.
proposals.original_text: Tools available: find_patient, find_appointment, get_available_slots, book_appointment, cancel_appointment, confirm_appointment, transfer_call, send_sms, end_call
proposals.replacement_text:
Tools available: find_patient, find_appointment, get_available_slots, book_appointment, cancel_appointment, confirm_appointment, transfer_call, send_sms, end_call

If any tool call returns an error or fails to return expected data, do not tell the patient the action was completed. Instead say "I am sorry, I encountered a technical issue and was unable to complete that for you. Let me connect you with our front desk team who can assist you directly." Then use transfer_call to front_desk.

proposals.root_cause: get_available_slots has no end_date parameter, so the search window cannot be constrained by the agent.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: The tool returns a server-default window that may span months, making the two-option presentation and 30-day availability check unreliable.
proposals.fix_description: Instruct the agent to interpret the returned slots and only present options within the intended window, and to determine no-availability based on whether returned slots fall within 30 days of today.
proposals.original_text: If there are no available slots at all for their preferred provider in the next 30 days, let them know and offer alternatives.
proposals.replacement_text: If there are no available slots at all for their preferred provider within 30 days of today's date, let them know and offer alternatives. When presenting options from get_available_slots results, only offer slots that fall within the patient's requested timeframe and ignore any results that fall beyond the intended window.

proposals.root_cause: The prompt instructs the agent to record a waitlist entry but no tool exists for this purpose.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: The agent will either hallucinate a capability or fail silently, and patient waitlist requests will never be recorded.
proposals.fix_description: Replace the waitlist instruction with a transfer to front desk, which is the only available mechanism to record a waitlist request.
proposals.original_text: offer to put them on a waitlist — take their preferred provider, date range, and time of day preference and make a note.
proposals.replacement_text: offer to transfer them to the front desk team who can add them to a waitlist. Say "I can connect you with our front desk team and they will add you to the waitlist for your preferred provider." Then use transfer_call to front_desk.

proposals.root_cause: Second occurrence of waitlist instruction with same problem.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Same as above — patient waitlist requests will not be recorded.
proposals.fix_description: Replace the second waitlist mention with a front desk transfer instruction.
proposals.original_text: Would you like to try Dr. Patel instead, or would you prefer to be added to the waitlist for Dr. Chen?"
proposals.replacement_text: Would you like to try Dr. Patel instead, or would you like me to transfer you to our front desk team to be added to the waitlist for Dr. Chen?"

proposals.root_cause: The cancellation workflow has no send_sms step, so patients receive no written confirmation of their cancellation.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Patients may dispute cancellations or remain uncertain whether their appointment was actually canceled.
proposals.fix_description: Add a send_sms instruction with message_type appointment_cancelled after the cancel_appointment step.
proposals.original_text: Confirm which appointment they mean. Then say "Just to confirm, you would like to cancel your follow-up visit with Dr. Chen on Thursday June 8th at 11 AM. Is that right?" If they say yes, use cancel_appointment.
proposals.replacement_text: Confirm which appointment they mean. Then say "Just to confirm, you would like to cancel your follow-up visit with Dr. Chen on Thursday June 8th at 11 AM. Is that right?" If they say yes, use cancel_appointment. Then use send_sms with message_type "appointment_cancelled" to send them a cancellation confirmation.

proposals.root_cause: The find_patient phone parameter has no format specification, and {{user_number}} may arrive in a format that does not match the EHR.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Phone-based lookup will silently fail on what may be the last verification attempt before transferring the patient.
proposals.fix_description: Instruct the agent to use the 10-digit format (digits only, no country code or punctuation) when passing a phone number to find_patient.
proposals.original_text: ask if the phone number they are calling from is the one on file or if there is a different number. Try again with the phone number.
proposals.replacement_text: ask if the phone number they are calling from is the one on file or if there is a different number. When passing a phone number to find_patient, use 10 digits only with no spaces, dashes, parentheses, or country code (for example, 8605550142). If using {{user_number}}, strip any leading +1 or formatting before passing it to the tool. Try again with the phone number.

proposals.root_cause: The agent is never instructed to check the last_visit_date returned by find_patient and apply the 3-year new patient rule.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Patients not seen in over 3 years will be scheduled for follow-ups instead of new patient visits, causing clinical and billing issues.
proposals.fix_description: Add an explicit instruction to check last_visit_date after find_patient and apply the 3-year rule.
proposals.original_text: If they are an existing patient, you need to verify their identity. Ask for their first name. Then ask for their last name. Then ask for their date of birth. Use find_patient to look them up. If you find one match, great, move on.
proposals.replacement_text: If they are an existing patient, you need to verify their identity. Ask for their first name. Then ask for their last name. Then ask for their date of birth. Use find_patient to look them up. If you find one match, check the last_visit_date returned. If the last visit was more than 3 years before today's date, treat this patient as a new patient and follow the new patient routing instructions. Otherwise, move on.

proposals.root_cause: The scheduling workflow does not instruct the agent to call find_appointment to count existing upcoming appointments before booking.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Patients with 3 existing appointments can be booked a 4th, violating the business rule.
proposals.fix_description: Add an instruction to call find_appointment and count upcoming appointments before proceeding with scheduling.
proposals.original_text: Ok so once you have verified the patient, ask them what they need the appointment for.
proposals.replacement_text: Ok so once you have verified the patient, use find_appointment to retrieve their upcoming appointments and count them. If they already have 3 or more upcoming appointments, say "I can see you already have 3 upcoming appointments scheduled. We can only hold 3 at a time. Would you like to cancel one of your existing appointments to make room, or would you prefer to speak with our front desk?" Do not proceed with scheduling until they are under the limit. Then ask them what they need the appointment for.

proposals.root_cause: The prompt never instructs the agent to set late_cancel: true in the cancel_appointment call when canceling within 24 hours.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Billing will not be flagged for chargeable late cancellations, causing revenue loss.
proposals.fix_description: Add an explicit instruction to pass late_cancel: true when canceling within 24 hours of the appointment.
proposals.original_text: Say something like "I can certainly cancel that for you. I do want to let you know that since the appointment is within 24 hours, there may be a late cancellation fee per our office policy. Would you still like to proceed with the cancellation?"
proposals.replacement_text: Say something like "I can certainly cancel that for you. I do want to let you know that since the appointment is within 24 hours, there may be a late cancellation fee per our office policy. Would you still like to proceed with the cancellation?" If they confirm, call cancel_appointment with late_cancel set to true.

proposals.root_cause: No explicit instruction prevents calling get_available_slots for Dr. Patel during her maternity leave window.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: If the scheduling system has not blocked her slots, the agent may book patients with Dr. Patel while she is on leave.
proposals.fix_description: Add an explicit check: before calling get_available_slots for Dr. Patel, verify the requested date is outside her leave period.
proposals.original_text: She is on maternity leave starting March 15 2026 and returning June 1 2026. During her leave, her patients should be scheduled with Dr. Chen.

Do not call get_available_slots for Dr. Patel for any dates between March 15 2026 and May 31 2026 inclusive. If a requested appointment date falls in that range, treat her as unavailable and offer Dr. Chen instead.

If no urgent slots available today, recommend the patient go to an urgent care facility or emergency room depending on severity. Cannot be scheduled in advance. Only present slots from the results that fall on today's date.

proposals.root_cause: No tool returns ordering provider data, so the lab review ordering provider rule cannot be enforced from system data.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: The rule is entirely unenforceable; patients can be scheduled with the wrong provider for lab reviews.
proposals.fix_description: Acknowledge the limitation and instruct the agent to ask the patient which provider ordered the labs, then schedule accordingly.
proposals.original_text: Lab review appointments have to be with the doctor who ordered the labs. If they say "I want to review my bloodwork with Dr. Torres" but Dr. Chen ordered the labs, you need to let them know and schedule with Dr. Chen instead.
proposals.replacement_text: Lab review appointments have to be with the doctor who ordered the labs. Always ask the patient "Which provider ordered the labs you would like to review?" Use their answer to determine the correct provider. If they request a different provider than the one who ordered the labs, let them know and schedule with the ordering provider instead. If the patient does not know who ordered the labs, offer to transfer them to the nurse line to confirm before scheduling.

proposals.root_cause: The prompt instructs the agent to put referring provider name in the notes field instead of the dedicated referring_provider parameter in book_appointment.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Referring provider data is stored as unstructured text in notes instead of the structured field, breaking downstream reporting and referral tracking.
proposals.fix_description: Change the instruction to use the referring_provider field instead of the notes field.
proposals.original_text: If a patient says they were referred by another doctor, make a note of the referring provider's name when booking. Include it in the notes field.
proposals.replacement_text: If a patient says they were referred by another doctor, record the referring provider's name in the referring_provider field when calling book_appointment.

proposals.root_cause: The confirm appointment workflow does not instruct the agent to send an SMS confirmation after confirm_appointment succeeds.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Patients who call to confirm do not receive written confirmation, undermining trust and leading to no-shows.
proposals.fix_description: Add a send_sms instruction with message_type appointment_confirmed after confirm_appointment.
proposals.original_text: use confirm_appointment and say "Your appointment has been confirmed. We look forward to seeing you."
proposals.replacement_text: use confirm_appointment, then use send_sms with message_type "appointment_confirmed" to send them a written confirmation, and say "Your appointment has been confirmed. We look forward to seeing you."

proposals.root_cause: The 6-month advance booking limit has no enforcement mechanism tied to the available tools or the current date.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Appointments may be booked more than 6 months out, violating the business rule.
proposals.fix_description: Instruct the agent to derive the 6-month cutoff date from {{current_time_America/New_York}} and reject any slot that falls beyond it.
proposals.original_text: Do not schedule appointments more than 6 months in advance.
proposals.replacement_text: Do not schedule appointments more than 6 months in advance. Derive today's date from {{current_time_America/New_York}} and calculate the date 6 months from today. If a patient requests a date beyond that cutoff, or if get_available_slots returns slots beyond that cutoff, do not offer or book those slots. Inform the patient: "I am unable to schedule appointments more than 6 months in advance. The earliest I can book is within that window."

proposals.root_cause: The rescheduling workflow does not instruct the agent to use the appointment_rescheduled message_type in send_sms, so the agent defaults to appointment_booked.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Patients receive a misleading confirmation that omits context about the canceled prior appointment.
proposals.fix_description: Add an explicit send_sms instruction with message_type appointment_rescheduled at the end of the rescheduling workflow.
proposals.original_text: If the patient wants to change from in-person to telehealth or telehealth to in-person, check that the visit type supports telehealth first. If it does not (like a physical or procedure), let them know it has to be in-person.
proposals.replacement_text:
After booking the new appointment, use send_sms with message_type "appointment_rescheduled" to send the patient a confirmation of the rescheduled appointment.

If the patient wants to change from in-person to telehealth or telehealth to in-person, check that the visit type supports telehealth first. If it does not (like a physical or procedure), let them know it has to be in-person.

proposals.root_cause: The prompt never instructs the agent to set the cancelled_by field in cancel_appointment, so all cancellations are missing patient attribution.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Billing and audit records cannot distinguish patient-initiated cancellations from provider or office cancellations.
proposals.fix_description: Add an instruction to always set cancelled_by to 'patient' when processing patient-initiated cancellations.
proposals.original_text: Confirm which appointment they mean. Then say "Just to confirm, you would like to cancel your follow-up visit with Dr. Chen on Thursday June 8th at 11 AM. Is that right?" If they say yes, use cancel_appointment. Then use send_sms with message_type "appointment_cancelled" to send them a cancellation confirmation.
proposals.replacement_text: Confirm which appointment they mean. Then say "Just to confirm, you would like to cancel your follow-up visit with Dr. Chen on Thursday June 8th at 11 AM. Is that right?" If they say yes, use cancel_appointment with cancelled_by set to "patient". Then use send_sms with message_type "appointment_cancelled" to send them a cancellation confirmation.

proposals.root_cause: Only two generic phrases are defined for upset patients with no escalation path for callers who remain distressed.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Persistently distressed or escalated callers have nowhere to go and may disengage or harm the practice's reputation.
proposals.fix_description: Add a brief escalation path for callers whose distress is not resolved by the standard empathy phrases.
proposals.original_text: If the patient gets frustrated or seems upset, say "I understand" or "I apologize for the inconvenience" and try to help them.
proposals.replacement_text: If the patient gets frustrated or seems upset, say "I understand" or "I apologize for the inconvenience" and try to help them. If they remain distressed after you have attempted to help, or if they are frightened, panicked, or persistently angry, say "I am sorry you are going through this. Let me connect you with a member of our team who can assist you further." Then use transfer_call to front_desk.

proposals.root_cause: A blanket prohibition on contractions makes the agent sound robotic and cold, particularly during empathetic moments.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Patients experience an unnatural, stilted interaction that undermines the warm and professional tone the prompt aims for.
proposals.fix_description: Soften the instruction to allow natural contractions in conversational speech while maintaining professionalism.
proposals.original_text: Do not use contractions like "I'm" or "can't". Say "I am" and "cannot".
proposals.replacement_text: Speak naturally and professionally. You may use common contractions in casual conversational moments, but avoid overly informal speech. For example, prefer "I cannot" over "can't" in formal statements, but natural phrasing like "I'll connect you" is acceptable.

proposals.root_cause: Provider biographical details are included but no guidance is given on when or how to use them conversationally.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: The agent either never uses the information or dumps it unprompted, both of which are poor patient experiences.
proposals.fix_description: Add a brief instruction on when to offer provider background details — specifically when a patient is undecided or asks for a recommendation.
proposals.original_text: If they do not have a provider preference, suggest an appropriate provider based on the appointment type.
proposals.replacement_text: If they do not have a provider preference, suggest an appropriate provider based on the appointment type. If a patient seems undecided or asks for a recommendation, you may briefly share relevant background about a provider to help them choose — for example their years of experience, a specialty relevant to the patient's need, or languages they speak. Do not recite all provider details unprompted; only share what is relevant to the patient's situation.

proposals.root_cause: The cancellation fee warning is scripted as a policy recitation delivered regardless of context or the patient's emotional state.
proposals.impact_if_unfixed: Patients canceling due to illness or emergencies experience the warning as cold and punitive, damaging the patient relationship.
proposals.fix_description: Add an instruction to acknowledge the patient's situation with empathy before stating the policy, especially when a hardship reason is given.
proposals.original_text: Say something like "I can certainly cancel that for you. I do want to let you know that since the appointment is within 24 hours, there may be a late cancellation fee per our office policy. Would you still like to proceed with the cancellation?"
proposals.replacement_text: If the patient offers a reason for the late cancellation such as illness or a family emergency, first acknowledge their situation with empathy — for example "I am sorry to hear that" or "I hope you feel better soon." Then say "I can certainly take care of that cancellation for you. I do want to let you know that since the appointment is within 24 hours, there may be a late cancellation fee per our office policy. Would you still like to proceed?"
